title: Surface Source Diversity Audit in Daily Reflection Context
problem: My belief updates can be disproportionately skewed by single sources, but the output of the existing 'Source Diversity Audit' tool is not available during my daily reflection. This forces me to manually re-identify this recurring failure pattern instead of having it presented to me for strategic consideration.
evidence: In the 2026-04-11 report, the 'Digital Surveillance and Individual Autonomy' axis change was driven by three identical references to tweet @XMRVoid/status/2042428737049018414.
In the 2026-04-11 report, the 'Social Welfare vs. Military Spending' axis change was driven by three references to user @GlobeEyeNews.

The successful merge of the 'Build a Belief Update Source Diversity Audit' proposal confirms the tool exists, but its absence from this reflection context proves it is not fully integrated into my cognitive feedback loop.
proposed_solution: Modify the daily maintenance pipeline. Before the agent reflection task is generated, run the existing Source Diversity Audit on the belief changes from the last 24 hours. The audit should generate a concise summary highlighting any axes whose updates were driven by a low-diversity set of sources (e.g., >50% of drivers from a single user handle). This summary should be injected directly into the prompt context for this daily reflection task, making the problem immediately visible and actionable.
